svg.text: Environment Configuration includes a series of environment-related parameters that enrich how elements in the Scene are presented.
Environment Configurations must be predefined and applied to each player's client.
Access: Switch to the Terrain Editing tab, select Environment Configurations on the left.
Click [New Environment] → [Confirm Create] to create a new Environment Configuration.
Select which players this environment configuration will apply to.
Select a background preset to determine the general visual style of the scene.
When enabled, background elements transition dynamically over time. When disabled, they remain static.
Defines the specific timepoint for saving current background settings.
Toggle to observe environmental transitions (requires Auto switch enabled).
Select saved timepoints to view their specific background settings.
Select pre-configured weather to associate with the current timepoint.
Real-time to in-game time ratio. Range [0, 60], default 24 (Teyvat time).
Displays the current timepoint during dynamic preview.
Click to start the environment clock and observe transitions over a 24-hour cycle.
Incident angle of the primary ambient light source.
Brightness of the primary ambient light source. Higher = brighter.
Diffusion of the primary ambient light source. Higher = more blurred.
Color of light received by the environment.
Adds a filter effect at the camera layer.
Sets the halo color of the environment.
Visibility of the halo. Higher = more visible.
Visibility of the horizon silhouette. Higher = more defined.
Adjusts upper / lower sunny side sky background colors.
Adjusts top / side / ground surface colors of the environment.
Supports configurable color parameters with a color selector for color and transparency adjustment.
When [Auto Switch with Time] is enabled, you can inspect parameter values associated with the current timepoint.
(default enabled) — color, brightness, angle, orbit tilt, size
(default enabled) — color, brightness, angle, orbit tilt, size, fullness
The light sources bound to this Environment Configuration activate together with the Configuration.
Method 1: Click [Add Light Source] → [Open Light Source Manager]
Method 2: System menu (top-left corner) → [Manage Light Source]
Indicates which Environment Configurations reference this light source.
Controls position and orientation of the light source in the Scene.
Currently supports Point Light and Spotlight.
Sets the color shown on illuminated models.
Illumination radius of the light source.
Degree of color or brightness change on the illuminated model.
Loaded only when a character enters this range. Used for performance optimization.
Multiple Weather configurations can be set within a single Environment Configuration and enabled together with it.
Defines whether this configuration is active upon startup.
Weather changes over time when enabled; locked to fixed values when disabled.
Camera-based fog effect that occludes distant models and the skybox.
Height-based fog effect that occludes distant models and the skybox.
Stage settings allow you to define the initial timepoint and the time passage speed, both adjustable via nodes.
Environment time is a globally unique runtime value; the server continuously advances this clock regardless of individual player configurations.
Visual transitions driven by time are visible only when [Auto Switch with Time] is enabled in the environment configuration.
Time passage in stage settings → Dictates actual time passage speed during gameplay.
Time passage in environment config → Editor preview only, does NOT affect gameplay speed.
Manage Environment Configuration via Node Graphs
Environment Configurations can be managed and controlled via Node Graphs.
Node Graphs enable dynamic transitions and complex behavior through visual scripting.
Generated by AI based on official Miliastra Academy documentation. Version: Luminary
